body.title: Extract AgentBase from IgorBase — generic base for any UC agent
body.result: AgentBase extracted to lab/utility_closet/agent_base.py. IgorBase is thin subclass. Configurable log_dir, get_timer() Igor-specific. All 20+ subclasses unchanged. 25 tests.
body.description:
Extract the generic parts of IgorBase into an AgentBase class that any UC agent can inherit. IgorBase becomes a thin subclass adding Igor-specific bits.

## Current IgorBase (wild_igor/igor/igor_base.py)
Provides: instance naming (GC lookup), lazy per-class logging, perf tracking (time_it, record_perf, perf_summary), debug utilities (dump, _get_caller). Zero required constructor args. Only coupling: paths() for log directory.

## New structure

### lab/utility_closet/agent_base.py — AgentBase
```python
class AgentBase:
    def __init__(self, log_dir: Path | None = None):
        self._perf_history: dict[str, list[float]] = defaultdict(list)
        self._log_dir = log_dir  # configurable, not hardcoded

    # Everything from IgorBase that's generic:
    def get_name(self) -> str          # GC reverse-lookup
    @property
    def log(self) -> Logger            # lazy per-class logger
    def time_it(self, label: str)      # context manager
    def record_perf(self, label, ms)   # write timing
    def _perf_summary(self, label)     # p50/p95/p99
    def dump(self)                     # debug __dict__
    def _get_caller(self, depth)       # stack introspection
```

### wild_igor/igor/igor_base.py — IgorBase(AgentBase)
```python
from lab.utility_closet.agent_base import AgentBase

class IgorBase(AgentBase):
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__(log_dir=paths().logs)
        # Any Igor-specific init
```

All 20+ existing IgorBase subclasses (Cortex, Thalamus, NarrativeEngine, WordGraph, TurnPipeline, BaseReasoner, etc.) continue to work unchanged — they inherit IgorBase which inherits AgentBase.

### lab/utility_closet/agent_client.py — AgentClient(AgentBase)
Refactor UtilityClosetClient into AgentClient:
```python
class AgentClient(AgentBase):
    def __init__(self, uc_url: str, log_dir: Path | None = None):
        super().__init__(log_dir=log_dir)
        self._uc_url = uc_url

    def register(self, agent_id, capabilities, callback_url) -> bool
    def deregister(self) -> bool
    def push_stats(self, stats: dict) -> bool
    def send_message(self, content, channel) -> bool  # channel-aware
    def poll_messages(self, channel) -> list
    def start_stats_pusher(self, stats_fn, interval)
```

Igor's UtilityClosetClient becomes:
```python
from lab.utility_closet.agent_client import AgentClient

class UtilityClosetClient(AgentClient):
    # Igor-specific overrides if any, otherwise just pass-through
```

## Tool registry split
ToolRegistry itself is already 100% AI-agnostic. Keep it in the UC:
- Move `wild_igor/igor/tools/registry.py` → `lab/utility_closet/tool_registry.py`
- Tool dataclass unchanged (name, description, parameters, fn)
- Igor's tools/__init__.py imports from new location
- Add `infra.tools` table: shared tool definitions that any agent can discover
- Igor registers his tools in both in-memory registry AND infra.tools
- UC agents can query infra.tools to discover available tools without importing Igor code

## Files
- NEW: lab/utility_closet/agent_base.py
- NEW: lab/utility_closet/agent_client.py
- MOVE: wild_igor/igor/tools/registry.py → lab/utility_closet/tool_registry.py
- MODIFY: wild_igor/igor/igor_base.py (thin subclass)
- MODIFY: wild_igor/igor/web/utility_closet_client.py (thin subclass)
- MODIFY: wild_igor/igor/tools/__init__.py (import path)
- All IgorBase subclasses: NO CHANGES needed

## Testing
- Existing IgorBase tests pass unchanged
- New AgentBase tests: get_name, logging, perf tracking with custom log_dir
- AgentClient tests: registration, stats push, message send/poll
- ToolRegistry tests: all existing + infra.tools persistence
- Import path tests: verify old imports still resolve

## Depends on
- T-uc-schema-three-namespaces (for infra.tools table)

## Updates
- This replaces T-uc-base-class (#402)
